dataset_name: Pakistan Automotive DTC Reference – Complete Vehicle Edition

vehicles.pak_note: Pakistan's best-selling car. AGS clutch codes are extremely common due to heavy stop-start city traffic in Karachi, Lahore, and Islamabad. K10C engine is sensitive to dirty MAF in dusty conditions.

dtc_codes.description: Intake Camshaft Position Timing Over-Advanced Bank 1
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: VVT timing deviation greater than 10° from target
diagnostic_procedure: Change engine oil if overdue – most common cause.
Check VVT OCV solenoid (clean screen filter inside solenoid).
Inspect timing chain tensioner for wear.
Perform solenoid activation test with scan tool.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Low oil level or old oil is #1 cause on Alto in Pakistan.

dtc_codes.description: Mass Air Flow Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: MAF output signal implausible relative to engine speed and throttle
diagnostic_procedure: Clean MAF sensor with approved MAF cleaner – do not touch wire element.
Inspect air filter for heavy clogging.
Check intake system for cracks between air box and throttle body.
Measure MAF output at idle (should be ~1.5–2 g/s on K10C).
dtc_codes.pak_note: #1 sensor fault on Alto in Pakistan – dusty roads clog MAF within 15,000 km.

dtc_codes.description: Mass Air Flow Sensor Circuit Low Input
diagnostic_procedure: Check MAF wiring harness for short to ground.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common after monsoon season – moisture enters MAF connector.

dtc_codes.description: Intake Air Temperature Sensor Circuit High Input
diagnostic_procedure: Check IAT sensor circuit (integrated in MAF on K10C) for open.
Inspect sensor connector for pulled-out pin.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common after MAF replacement if connector not fully seated.

dtc_codes.description: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor Circuit Low Input
diagnostic_procedure: Check ECT sensor circuit for short to ground.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Watch for overheating in Pakistan summer – check coolant level first.

diagnostic_procedure: Check for vacuum leaks at intake manifold gasket, hoses, and throttle body.
Check fuel pressure at fuel rail.
On CNG-converted Altos: check CNG mixer air ratio and gas pressure regulator.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Very common on CNG-converted Alto. CNG mixer calibration drifts in heat.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Crankshaft RPM variation consistent with misfire on multiple cylinders
diagnostic_procedure: Replace all 3 spark plugs with OEM NGK plugs (not cheap copies).
Check ignition coil pack (K10C uses single COP coil per cylinder).
On CNG Altos: reduce spark plug gap slightly (0.7mm for CNG vs 1.0mm petrol).
dtc_codes.pak_note: Most common CEL code on Alto. Cheap aftermarket spark plugs are a major cause.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Crankshaft RPM variation consistent with cyl 1 misfire
diagnostic_procedure: Test cylinder 1 ignition coil – swap with cyl 2 or 3.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: RPM variation consistent with cyl 2 misfire
RPM variation consistent with cyl 3 misfire

dtc_codes.description: Camshaft Position Sensor Circuit Malfunction Bank 1
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: No camshaft position signal or signal out of sync with crankshaft
diagnostic_procedure: Check CMP sensor wiring and connector.
Measure sensor air gap to trigger wheel (0.5–1.5 mm).
Replace sensor if no output signal.

dtc_codes.description: Catalyst System Efficiency Below Threshold Bank 1
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Downstream O2 switching rate exceeds 40% of upstream rate
diagnostic_procedure: Check for misfire damage to catalyst.
Verify upstream O2 sensor is switching correctly.
Inspect catalytic converter for damage or lead poisoning.
Replace catalytic converter if efficiency confirmed low.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common >100,000 km. Low-quality petrol additives in Pakistan can poison catalyst.

dtc_codes.description: Evaporative Emission Control System Incorrect Purge Flow
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Purge flow not detected when valve commanded open
diagnostic_procedure: Check EVAP purge solenoid valve and vacuum line.
Test solenoid resistance (~30 Ω) and ECM control signal.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: EVAP system pressure loss > 1.0 inch H2O per minute
diagnostic_procedure: Check fuel cap – loose cap is #1 cause.
Inspect EVAP lines and canister for cracks.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Very common – loose fuel cap after refuelling at Pakistan petrol stations.

dtc_codes.description: EVAP Leak – Fuel Cap Loose or Off
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Fuel cap not generating proper seal pressure
diagnostic_procedure: Tighten or replace fuel cap – almost always the fix.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Most common EVAP code – tighten cap first.

dtc_codes.description: Idle Air Control System RPM Too Low
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Actual idle RPM more than 100 RPM below target
diagnostic_procedure: Clean throttle body bore and ISC passage with throttle body cleaner.
Perform throttle body adaptation (ECU reset or scan tool procedure).
dtc_codes.pak_note: Very common after 30,000 km – carbon from low-quality petrol.
Common in Pakistan summers – batteries degrade faster in heat.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Clutch actuator motor current or position out of range
diagnostic_procedure: Check AGS clutch actuator motor connector and wiring.
Perform AGS calibration/initialisation procedure with scan tool.
Inspect clutch actuator for mechanical seizure.
Check AGS ECU relay and power supply.
dtc_codes.pak_note: VERY COMMON in Pakistan – stop-start city driving wears AGS clutch actuator faster. Do AGS clutch point learning every 30,000 km.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Gear shift actuator position sensor out of range or motor failure
diagnostic_procedure: Check gear shift actuator motor and position sensor.
Perform AGS gear position calibration with scan tool.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common after impact damage to gear selector mechanism.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Clutch engagement point adaptation out of specification
diagnostic_procedure: Perform AGS clutch touch point learning procedure.
Check clutch wear – AGS clutch wears faster in Pakistan traffic.
Inspect clutch hydraulic actuator for fluid leakage.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Clutch lifespan on AGS is shorter in Pakistan due to city traffic.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Gear position sensor signal out of range
diagnostic_procedure: Check gear position sensor wiring and output voltage in each gear.

vehicles.pak_note: Premium hatchback version of Alto platform. Same K10B engine as WagonR. AGS faults and MAF sensor issues identical to Alto.

dtc_codes.description: Intake Camshaft Position Timing Over-Advanced Bank 1
dtc_codes.pak_note: Change oil on schedule – oil neglect is primary cause.

dtc_codes.description: Mass Air Flow Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: MAF output implausible for given throttle/RPM
diagnostic_procedure: Check intake system for air leaks.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Dusty Pakistan roads make this the #1 fault on Cultus.
CNG conversion lean faults are common on Cultus.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: RPM fluctuation consistent with multi-cylinder misfire
diagnostic_procedure: Replace spark plugs (NGK DILKAR6A11H OEM equivalent).
On CNG: check CNG injectors and gas pressure.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Cheap spark plugs are #1 cause in Pakistan.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Knock sensor signal absent or noisy
diagnostic_procedure: Check knock sensor mounting torque (20 Nm).
Test sensor resistance (should be infinite – piezo sensor).
dtc_codes.pak_note: Low-octane petrol can cause genuine knock – use RON92+ fuel.

dtc_codes.description: Catalyst System Efficiency Below Threshold Bank 1
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Downstream/upstream O2 switching ratio out of spec
diagnostic_procedure: Replace catalytic converter if confirmed low efficiency.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common >100,000 km with low-quality fuel.

diagnostic_procedure: Check purge solenoid and vacuum line.

dtc_codes.pak_note: Loose fuel cap – most common.

dtc_codes.description: Idle Air Control System RPM Too Low
dtc_codes.pak_note: Carbon buildup from poor-quality petrol.
Very common in Lahore/Karachi traffic.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Clutch engagement point out of spec
diagnostic_procedure: AGS clutch touch point re-learning required.

vehicles.pak_note: Same drivetrain as Cultus. Tall body creates more vibration which can loosen sensor connectors. Popular CNG conversion target.

dtc_codes.description: Mass Air Flow Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
dtc_codes.pak_note: Dusty roads – clean MAF every 15,000 km.

diagnostic_procedure: Check CNG kit calibration if installed.
dtc_codes.pak_note: CNG lean codes very common – most WagonR in Pakistan run on CNG.

diagnostic_procedure: Inspect CNG injectors if CNG kit installed.
Reduce spark plug gap to 0.7 mm for CNG operation.
dtc_codes.pak_note: #1 code on WagonR. CNG spark plug gap is critical.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: No CMP signal or out of sync
dtc_codes.pak_note: Vibration from rough roads can crack CMP sensor harness.

dtc_codes.description: Idle Control System RPM Too Low
dtc_codes.pak_note: Carbon buildup common.
Very common – heavy city traffic.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Clutch engagement point out of spec

vehicles.pak_note: Older carburettor-based or early TBI engine. Limited OBD-II coverage. Primary diagnostics are fuel delivery, ignition, and mechanical. Very widely used for commercial transport in Pakistan.

diagnostic_procedure: Replace spark plugs (NGK BPR5ES or equivalent).
Check fuel delivery – carburettor or TBI injector.
Inspect distributor cap and rotor (older models).
dtc_codes.pak_note: Extremely common on high-mileage Bolans. Always check spark plugs first.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Lean condition detected by O2 sensor
diagnostic_procedure: Inspect intake gasket for air leaks.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Carburettor jet clogging is very common with Pakistan petrol. Use carb cleaner.

diagnostic_procedure: Check and adjust carburettor float level.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Rich condition common with worn choke mechanism.

dtc_codes.description: O2 Sensor Circuit Malfunction Bank 1 Sensor 1
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: O2 sensor not switching or out of range
diagnostic_procedure: Check O2 sensor wiring and connector.
Replace O2 sensor if >80,000 km or aged.
Verify exhaust system for leaks near sensor.
dtc_codes.pak_note: O2 sensor replacement at 80,000 km recommended.

diagnostic_procedure: Check CMP sensor wiring in distributor.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Old distributor wear common on high-mileage Bolans.
VSS failure common on older Bolans. Affects speedometer too.
Old batteries and alternator failure common on Bolans used commercially.

vehicles.pak_note: Pakistan's #2 best-selling car. Very reliable but common issues include VVT faults from oil neglect, catalytic converter degradation, and oxygen sensor failure at higher mileage. Many Pakistan Corollas run on CNG.

dtc_codes.description: Intake (A) Camshaft Position Actuator Circuit Open Bank 1
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: OCV solenoid circuit resistance abnormal or no signal
diagnostic_procedure: Check Oil Control Valve (OCV) wiring – pin 1 (B+) and pin 2 (ECM signal).
Test ECM OCV output with scan tool (duty cycle 0–100%).
Inspect wiring harness for chafing near valve cover.

dtc_codes.description: Intake (A) Camshaft Position Timing Over-Advanced Bank 1
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: VVT phase angle > target by more than 8°
diagnostic_procedure: Change engine oil immediately if overdue.
Clean OCV solenoid screen (remove solenoid, clean screen with parts cleaner).
Check timing chain stretch – measure chain wear with special tool.
Verify oil pressure at idle (minimum 29 PSI warm).
dtc_codes.pak_note: Most common VVT fault on Corolla in Pakistan – oil change neglect is primary cause. OCV screen clogging from old oil is very common.

dtc_codes.description: Intake (A) Camshaft Position Timing Over-Retarded Bank 1
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: VVT phase angle < target by more than 8°

dtc_codes.description: Exhaust (B) Camshaft Position Timing Over-Advanced Bank 1
dtc_codes.pak_note: 1.8 Altis with Dual VVT-i only.

dtc_codes.description: Exhaust (B) Camshaft Position Timing Over-Retarded Bank 1
dtc_codes.pak_note: 1.8 Altis only.

dtc_codes.description: Crankshaft/Camshaft Position Correlation Bank 1 Sensor A
diagnostic_procedure: Verify CKP and CMP sensors are correctly installed.
If recently serviced: recheck timing chain installation.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common on high-mileage Corollas with stretched timing chain.

dtc_codes.description: Mass Air Flow Sensor Circuit Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Measure supply voltage (12V pin) and 5V reference.

dtc_codes.description: Mass Air Flow Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: MAF output implausible for engine conditions
diagnostic_procedure: Clean MAF hot wire with approved cleaner.
Inspect for intake air leaks between MAF and throttle body.
Verify no air leaks at MAF housing seal.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common in dusty Pakistan conditions. Clean MAF at every oil change.

dtc_codes.description: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor A Circuit Range/Performance
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: TPS signal deviation between sensor 1 and sensor 2 > 0.5V
diagnostic_procedure: Perform throttle position sensor initialisation (turn IG-ON, wait 2 sec, IG-OFF, wait 10 sec).
Check throttle body for carbon sticking plate.
Clean throttle body bore with cleaner.
If TPS replaced: perform throttle body calibration.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Carbon from poor-quality petrol is common cause. Clean throttle body every 40,000 km.

dtc_codes.description: O2 Sensor Circuit Malfunction Bank 1 Sensor 1
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: A/F sensor voltage out of range or not switching
diagnostic_procedure: Check upstream AF sensor (A/F sensor on Corolla, not standard O2).
Measure sensor resistance and heater circuit.

dtc_codes.description: O2 Sensor Circuit Slow Response Bank 1 Sensor 1
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Upstream sensor switching slower than specification
diagnostic_procedure: Replace upstream A/F sensor if >120,000 km.
Check for exhaust leaks near sensor.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common on high-mileage Pakistan Corollas.

dtc_codes.description: O2 Sensor Circuit Malfunction Bank 1 Sensor 2
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Downstream O2 sensor signal out of range
diagnostic_procedure: Replace sensor if beyond service life.
On CNG Corollas: check CNG ECU and injector calibration.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Very common on CNG-converted Corollas. CNG pressure regulator issues cause lean codes.

diagnostic_procedure: Replace spark plugs with NGK ILFR6B (1ZR) or ILFR6A11 (2ZR).
Check ignition coils – swap to identify faulty coil.
On CNG: reduce gap to 0.7 mm, check gas pressure.
Swap coil 1 to another cylinder to isolate.
Check cyl 1 injector resistance and spray.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Knock sensor signal absent or out of range
diagnostic_procedure: Check knock sensor wiring harness (runs under intake manifold).
Measure sensor resistance (should be infinite – piezo type).
Verify sensor torque to spec (25 Nm).
Replace sensor if wiring confirmed good but no signal.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Low-octane petrol causes genuine knock. Always use RON92+.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: No CKP signal or signal below threshold
diagnostic_procedure: Check CKP sensor (bottom of engine block).
Measure sensor air gap to flywheel ring gear (0.8–1.3 mm).
Check wiring harness for damage from heat or road debris.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common on Corollas exposed to road flooding during monsoon.

dtc_codes.description: Camshaft Position Sensor Circuit Malfunction Bank 1
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: No CMP signal or out of sync with CKP
diagnostic_procedure: Check CMP sensor (on valve cover, camshaft end).
Test sensor wiring – 3-wire Hall effect sensor.

dtc_codes.description: Camshaft Position Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Compare CMP vs CKP timing with scan tool.

dtc_codes.description: Catalyst System Efficiency Below Threshold Bank 1
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Post-cat O2 switch rate > 58% of pre-cat rate
diagnostic_procedure: Check downstream O2 sensor for accuracy.
Inspect catalytic converter for physical damage or clogging.
Replace catalytic converter (OEM or DensoToyota spec).
dtc_codes.pak_note: Very common >100,000 km. Lead additives in cheap Pakistan petrol can poison catalyst.

diagnostic_procedure: Check EVAP purge VSV (Vacuum Switching Valve).
Test VSV resistance (~30 Ω) and ECM duty cycle.

dtc_codes.pak_note: Canister flooding can occur if car is filled fuel over neck repeatedly.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: No or implausible vehicle speed signal
diagnostic_procedure: Check ABS wheel speed sensor signals.
Check ATF level and condition (should be clean red/pink).
dtc_codes.pak_note: Neglected ATF service is very common in Pakistan. Change ATF every 60,000 km.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Actual gear ratio differs from expected
diagnostic_procedure: Change ATF with Toyota T-IV or WS spec fluid.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Extremely common in Pakistan due to neglected ATF service.

diagnostic_procedure: Change ATF – contaminated fluid causes TCC slip.
dtc_codes.pak_note: ATF neglect is root cause in most Pakistan cases.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Shift solenoid A resistance or function fault
diagnostic_procedure: Check solenoid resistance (11–15 Ω for Toyota U340/U440 trans).

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Igniter primary circuit fault on coil group 1-2
diagnostic_procedure: Check ignition coil 1 and coil 2 wiring.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Igniter primary circuit fault on coil group 3-4
diagnostic_procedure: Check ignition coil 3 and coil 4 wiring.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: ECM detects start failure or difficult start condition
diagnostic_procedure: Verify CKP sensor signal on start.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common when ignition switch wears out on older Corollas.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: PNP switch signal inconsistent with selected gear
diagnostic_procedure: Test switch continuity in each gear position (P/N should close starter circuit).
Adjust switch if out of calibration.

vehicles.pak_note: Newer platform than Corolla. 1NR-FE and 2NR-FE are reliable engines. Common issues are MAF sensor cleaning, O2 sensor, and transmission fluid maintenance.

dtc_codes.pak_note: Oil maintenance is critical.
Same MAF cleaning requirement as Corolla.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: ECT not reaching normal operating temperature
dtc_codes.pak_note: Important in Pakistan winters – cold ECT affects fuel mixture.

diagnostic_procedure: Spark plug, coil, injector, compression check.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression check.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression check.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression check.

dtc_codes.pak_note: Keep ATF fresh on 4AT.

vehicles.pak_note: Tenth-gen 1.5T Civic is popular in Pakistan upper middle class. VTEC and oil dilution issues on 1.5T are known globally. R18A on older gen is very reliable but VTEC codes from oil neglect are common.

dtc_codes.description: Intake (A) Camshaft Position Timing Over-Advanced Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Change engine oil (0W-20 for 1.5T, 5W-30 for R18A).
Perform VTC actuator test with scan tool.

dtc_codes.description: Exhaust (B) Camshaft Position Timing Over-Advanced Bank 1
dtc_codes.pak_note: 1.5T only – has dual VTC.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Oil pressure switch does not confirm VTEC activation or oil pressure < 40 PSI during VTEC activation attempt
diagnostic_procedure: Check oil level and pressure first.
Inspect VTEC solenoid spool valve filter for sludge clogging.
Clean VTEC spool valve with solvent.
Test VTEC oil pressure switch (ground to chassis when VTEC active).
Change engine oil if old – this is #1 cause in Pakistan.
Verify oil meets Honda spec (Honda Genuine Motor Oil or equivalent).
dtc_codes.pak_note: MOST COMMON code on Civic in Pakistan. Neglected oil changes clog VTEC spool valve filter. Change oil every 5,000 km with full-synthetic Honda spec oil.

diagnostic_procedure: Check MAP sensor circuit for short to ground.
Check EVAP purge valve (common Honda lean cause).
On 1.5T: check for oil dilution from petrol (known issue) causing lean condition.
dtc_codes.pak_note: 1.5T oil dilution with petrol is known in cold conditions – check oil level and odour.

diagnostic_procedure: Check EVAP purge valve for stuck open (very common Honda rich cause).
dtc_codes.pak_note: EVAP purge valve sticking open is common Honda rich cause.

diagnostic_procedure: Replace spark plugs (Honda IZFR6K11 or NGK equivalent).
On 1.5T: check for oil/water in cylinders from head gasket.

dtc_codes.pak_note: 1.5T only.

diagnostic_procedure: Check intercooler piping for cracks (common in Pakistan heat cycling).
dtc_codes.pak_note: 1.5T only. Intercooler pipe cracks common.

dtc_codes.description: EVAP System Leak Detected – Fuel Tank Side
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: EVAP pressure loss detected from fuel tank side
diagnostic_procedure: Inspect fuel tank EVAP lines and joints.
Smoke test EVAP from fuel tank side.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common after bad refuelling cap replacement.

dtc_codes.description: EVAP System Leak Detected – Canister Side
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: EVAP pressure loss from canister side
diagnostic_procedure: Check charcoal canister and vent valve.
Inspect canister to purge valve hoses.
Scan CVT TCM for specific codes.
Check CVT fluid level (Honda HCF-2 spec).
dtc_codes.pak_note: Change CVT fluid (Honda HCF-2) every 40,000 km.

diagnostic_procedure: Change CVT fluid with Honda HCF-2.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Old/wrong CVT fluid causes this.

vehicles.pak_note: Most popular Honda in Pakistan. L15A is a very reliable engine but VTEC faults from oil neglect are common. CVT issues from neglected fluid are widespread.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Oil pressure switch does not confirm VTEC engagement
diagnostic_procedure: Check engine oil level and condition.
Inspect VTEC solenoid spool valve for sludge (remove and clean filter screen).
Test oil pressure switch – should ground at VTEC engagement.
Change oil with Honda Genuine 5W-30 or equivalent full synthetic.
Verify oil pressure at idle and at VTEC activation RPM (~2500 RPM).
dtc_codes.pak_note: #1 Honda City fault code in Pakistan. Oil change neglect is almost always the cause. Use full-synthetic Honda spec oil.
Very common – clean MAF every oil change in Pakistan.

dtc_codes.description: Intake Air Temperature Sensor Circuit High
diagnostic_procedure: Check IAT sensor (in MAF unit) circuit for open.
Check ECT sensor for short to ground.
Check EVAP purge valve for stuck open.
dtc_codes.pak_note: EVAP purge valve sticking open is common Honda rich code cause.

diagnostic_procedure: Replace spark plugs (NGK IZFR5K11 for 1.2, IZFR6K11 for 1.5).
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression on cyl 1.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression on cyl 2.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression on cyl 3.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression on cyl 4.
Check CMP sensor wiring and supply.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Fuel tank side EVAP pressure loss

diagnostic_procedure: Check Honda HCF-2 CVT fluid level and condition.
Replace fluid if dark or >40,000 km.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Honda CVT fluid must be HCF-2 spec. Wrong fluid destroys CVT.

dtc_codes.description: IAC Valve Circuit Failure Low (older City L13A)
diagnostic_procedure: Check IAC valve wiring on older L13A model.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Older City (pre-2009) only.

vehicles.pak_note: Pakistan still sells the pre-facelift Swift. M13A is reliable but common issues are MAF sensor, O2 sensor, and 4AT transmission solenoids.

dtc_codes.pak_note: Dusty conditions – clean MAF regularly.

diagnostic_procedure: Spark plug, coil, injector, compression cyl 1.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression cyl 2.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression cyl 3.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression cyl 4.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Knock sensor signal absent or noisy
diagnostic_procedure: Check knock sensor wiring under intake manifold.
Replace sensor if wiring confirmed good.
Check CKP sensor wiring and resistance.
Inspect air gap to ring gear.

dtc_codes.pak_note: 4AT only. ATF neglect very common.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: EGR position sensor signal out of range
dtc_codes.pak_note: EGR carbon clogging from low-quality petrol.

vehicles.pak_note: Relatively new to Pakistan. Kappa 1.0L 3-cyl is a modern engine with good reliability track record. Common issues are throttle body carbon, O2 sensor, and CVVT faults.

dtc_codes.description: Intake Camshaft Position Actuator Circuit Open Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Test ECM CVVT output duty cycle.

dtc_codes.pak_note: Oil maintenance critical on Kia Kappa engine.

diagnostic_procedure: Perform throttle body adaptation (IG-ON wait sequence).
Replace spark plugs (NGK ILFR5B11 or equivalent).
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression cyl 1.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression cyl 2.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression cyl 3.
dtc_codes.pak_note: 1.0L 3-cyl only.

vehicles.pak_note: Budget Chinese sedan competing with Suzuki and Toyota entry cars. Common issues include CVT reliability concerns, ECM software glitches, and sensor faults. Requires Changan-compatible scan tool for full diagnostics.

dtc_codes.pak_note: Use Changan/Geely spec iridium plugs.

diagnostic_procedure: Spark plug, coil, injector, compression cyl 1.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression cyl 2.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression cyl 3.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression cyl 4.
Check CKP sensor wiring and gap.
Check CVT fluid level and condition.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Changan CVT reliability concerns in Pakistan – change CVT fluid every 40,000 km strictly.
Wrong CVT fluid type will damage CVT.

diagnostic_procedure: Reflash ECM with latest Changan software.
Check ECM power supply and ground.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Chinese brand ECMs sometimes need reflashing for software bugs.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Module communication loss on proprietary CAN network
diagnostic_procedure: Check all module CAN bus connectors.
Inspect CAN bus wiring for damage.
Use Changan diagnostic tool for full details.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Requires Changan-specific OBD tool. Generic tools may not read this code.

vehicles.pak_note: Popular compact SUV assembled in Pakistan. Nu 2.0 engine is reliable. Common issues are CVVT, MAF sensor, and ATF maintenance.

dtc_codes.description: Intake (A) Camshaft Position Actuator Circuit Open Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Check CVVT solenoid wiring and connector.
Clean CVVT oil control valve filter.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Oil quality critical – use 0W-20 full synthetic.
Nu engine has dual CVVT.

diagnostic_procedure: Replace iridium spark plugs (OEM Hyundai/Kia spec).

dtc_codes.pak_note: Change ATF (Kia SP-IV or equivalent) every 60,000 km.
ATF neglect causes gear ratio faults.

vehicles.pak_note: Fast-growing SUV segment in Pakistan. Nu 2.0L is the primary engine. CRITICAL: Theta II 2.4L engine (some imports) has P1326 bearing wear code – stop engine immediately if this code appears.

dtc_codes.description: Intake (A) Camshaft Position Actuator Circuit Open
KSDS – Knock Sensor Detection System Engine Bearing Wear ⚠️ CRITICAL
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Knock sensor detects abnormal vibration pattern consistent with connecting rod bearing wear
diagnostic_procedure: STOP ENGINE IMMEDIATELY – serious internal engine damage indicated.
Check engine oil level – low oil is primary cause.
Have engine professionally inspected for rod/main bearing wear.
If bearings confirmed worn, engine rebuild or replacement required.
File for Theta II engine recall (Hyundai global recall – check if applicable).
dtc_codes.pak_note: ⚠️ CRITICAL FAULT – Applies to Theta II 2.4L engine variants. This is part of the global Hyundai/Kia Theta II recall issue. If Tucson has 2.4L engine and shows P1326 – stop driving immediately. Very serious fault related to oil system failure causing rod bearing wear.

dtc_codes.description: Left Front Wheel Speed Sensor Malfunction
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: LF wheel speed signal absent or erratic
diagnostic_procedure: Check LF wheel speed sensor wiring.
Check for road debris on sensor tip.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common on rough Pakistan roads. Wheel speed sensors get damaged by potholes.

dtc_codes.description: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check RF sensor and tone ring.

dtc_codes.description: Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check LR sensor and rear axle wiring.

dtc_codes.description: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Malfunction

vehicles.pak_note: Fastest-growing SUV in Pakistan. DCT transmission is a known concern – requires careful adaptation and fluid maintenance. GW4B15A 1.5T timing chain tensioner wear has been reported by Pakistan owners.

dtc_codes.description: Crankshaft/Camshaft Position Correlation Bank 1 Sensor A
diagnostic_procedure: Inspect timing chain tensioner for wear – known issue on early Pakistan GW4B15A units.
Verify CKP and CMP sensors are correctly installed.
Replace timing chain and tensioner if worn.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Reported timing chain issue on Haval H6 Pakistan units. Check at first signs of rattling at cold start.

dtc_codes.description: Intake Camshaft Position Timing Over-Advanced Bank 1
dtc_codes.pak_note: Use GWM-specified 0W-20 oil.

diagnostic_procedure: Check intercooler pipes for cracks (heat cycling in Pakistan).
dtc_codes.pak_note: Intercooler pipe cracks common in Pakistan summer heat.

diagnostic_procedure: Replace OEM GWM spec iridium spark plugs.
Check turbo boost on all cylinders.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression cyl 1.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression cyl 2.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression cyl 3.
Spark plug, coil, injector, compression cyl 4.
Check CMP sensor wiring and output.
Check DCT fluid level and condition (GWM DCT fluid spec).
dtc_codes.pak_note: 7DCT is the most discussed component on Haval H6 in Pakistan. Use only GWM-specified DCT fluid. Perform DCT adaptation after fluid change.
DCT fluid change interval is 40,000 km.

dtc_codes.description: Torque Converter Clutch Performance (DCT Clutch Slip)
dtc_codes.pak_note: DCT clutch slip is commonly reported in heavy Pakistan city traffic – DCT adaptation required.

dtc_codes.description: Left Front Wheel Speed Sensor Malfunction
diagnostic_procedure: Check LF sensor and tone ring.

dtc_codes.description: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor Malfunction

vehicles.pak_note: Premium SUV – aspirational vehicle in Pakistan. Both petrol and diesel variants popular. 1GD-FTV diesel has EGR clogging issues with low-quality Pakistan diesel. 2TR-FE has VVT-i related codes.

diagnostic_procedure: Clean VVT OCV solenoid filter screen.
dtc_codes.pak_note: 2TR-FE petrol only. Oil neglect is primary cause.
2TR-FE petrol only.
V6 2TR-FE Bank 2.

diagnostic_procedure: Replace OEM iridium spark plugs (all 6 cylinders on V6).

dtc_codes.pak_note: V6 only.
V6 only.

dtc_codes.description: Fuel Rail/System Pressure Too Low (1GD-FTV Diesel)
diagnostic_procedure: Check fuel filter condition – replace if clogged.
Test common rail pressure regulator valve.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Pakistan diesel quality varies – use filtered diesel. Fuel filter should be changed every 20,000 km.

diagnostic_procedure: Compare sensor reading with actual pressure.

dtc_codes.description: Fuel Rail Pressure Sensor Circuit High (1GD-FTV)
diagnostic_procedure: Check sensor circuit for short to battery.
Scan for specific injector cylinder codes.
Use Toyota Techstream to test individual injector balance rates.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common rail diesel injector issues arise from dirty/contaminated Pakistan diesel.

diagnostic_procedure: Check cyl 2 injector resistance and wiring.
Check cyl 3 injector resistance and wiring.
Check cyl 4 injector resistance and wiring.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: EGR flow not within expected range
diagnostic_procedure: Inspect EGR valve and passages for heavy carbon deposit – very common in Pakistan.
Clean EGR valve with EGR cleaner spray.
Test EGR actuator operation with scan tool.
dtc_codes.pak_note: EGR carbon clogging is VERY COMMON on Fortuner diesel in Pakistan – poor quality diesel accelerates carbon buildup. Clean EGR every 40,000 km.

diagnostic_procedure: Inspect EGR cooler for soot blockage.
Replace EGR valve if cleaning does not resolve.
dtc_codes.pak_note: EGR cleaning is a routine service in Pakistan for Fortuner diesel.

diagnostic_procedure: Check EGR valve for sticking in open position.

dtc_codes.description: Turbocharger/Supercharger Boost Control Solenoid Circuit Range/Performance
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Boost solenoid signal out of range

diagnostic_procedure: Check for oil burning causing catalyst contamination.
Check 6AT fluid (Toyota WS or T-IV spec).
dtc_codes.pak_note: Keep ATF fresh – change every 60,000 km.

diagnostic_procedure: Adjust switch position if out of spec.

vehicles.pak_note: VERY POPULAR JDM import. Typically 5–10 years old when imported to Pakistan. The #1 issue is HV battery degradation – most Pakistan Aquas have weak battery modules. Requires Toyota Techstream or hybrid-compatible scanner.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: One or more HV battery modules have voltage deviation > 0.6V from pack average, or capacity < 60% of new
diagnostic_procedure: Scan HV battery ECU for individual module voltages.
Identify weak modules (low voltage or high resistance).
Replace individual modules or full HV battery pack.
Perform HV battery capacity test with Toyota Techstream.
After replacement: reset SOC learning values.
dtc_codes.pak_note: #1 code on Aqua in Pakistan. Almost ALL imported Aquas will show this eventually – battery modules degrade with age and heat. Module replacement (not full pack) is common practice in Pakistan.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: MG2 traction motor performance below specification
diagnostic_procedure: Check MG2 motor wiring and inverter.
Test inverter coolant system (check coolant level in hybrid inverter circuit).
Scan inverter control ECU for sub-codes.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Inverter overheating in Pakistan summer is a cause. Check inverter coolant pump.

dtc_codes.description: Hybrid Battery Pack Cooling Fan Malfunction
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Battery cooling fan motor circuit fault or fan not reaching target speed
diagnostic_procedure: Check HV battery cooling fan under rear seat.
Clean fan intake duct – commonly blocked with dust in Pakistan.
dtc_codes.pak_note: VERY COMMON in Pakistan – dust from roads clogs battery cooling fan intake. Clean fan duct every 20,000 km. Fan failure causes battery overheating and rapid degradation.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Engine output request cannot be satisfied by hybrid system
diagnostic_procedure: Check HV battery SOC – charge if depleted.
Check 12V auxiliary battery – failing 12V battery causes this code.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Weak 12V battery (under bonnet) can cause this. Change 12V battery every 3 years in Pakistan heat.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Engine unable to start in hybrid mode
diagnostic_procedure: Check engine startup components (starter motor relay in hybrid system).
dtc_codes.pak_note: 12V battery failure is most common cause in Pakistan.

diagnostic_procedure: Note: Hybrid engines run more cycles at lower RPM – misfires affect HV battery charging.
Replace catalytic converter if confirmed low.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common on older imported Aquas.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Immobiliser ECU cannot verify key transponder
diagnostic_procedure: Re-register key with immobiliser using Toyota Techstream.
Verify correct key for this VIN.
If JDM import: original Japanese owner transponder may need re-registration.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Very common on imported Aquas – immobiliser needs re-registration after ownership change/import. Must use Toyota Techstream.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: ECM not responding on CAN bus
diagnostic_procedure: Check ECM power supply and ground.
Inspect CAN bus wiring from ECM.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Weak 12V battery causes CAN faults on Aqua.

vehicles.pak_note: Pakistan's most common luxury/hybrid import. Gen 3 (2010–2015) is most prevalent. HV battery degradation is extremely common on Pakistan Prius units which are typically 8–12 years old. Requires Toyota Techstream for full diagnostics.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: HV battery module voltage deviation or capacity below threshold
diagnostic_procedure: Scan HV battery ECU with Toyota Techstream.
Check individual cell/module voltages (28 modules on Gen 3 Prius, should be within 0.3V of each other).
Replace weak modules or full battery pack.
After replacement: reset HV battery learning values.
Check HV battery cooling fan and duct.
dtc_codes.pak_note: THE most common Prius code in Pakistan. Most Pakistan Prius are 8–12 years old with degraded battery. Individual module replacement (not full pack) is common and economical practice. Expect 2–5 weak modules in Pakistan climate.

dtc_codes.description: Hybrid Battery Pack Cooling Fan Malfunction
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Battery cooling fan circuit fault or insufficient airflow
diagnostic_procedure: Locate cooling fan intake (passenger rear footwell area on Gen 3 Prius).
Clear all dust from fan intake screen.
Test fan motor for rotation and current draw.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Extremely common in Pakistan – dust blocks cooling fan. Fan failure leads to battery overheating and rapid cell death. Clean fan intake every 15,000 km.

diagnostic_procedure: Check MG2 motor wiring and inverter connections.
Inspect inverter coolant circuit (separate from engine coolant).
dtc_codes.pak_note: Inverter overheating in Pakistan summer (45°C+) is a cause.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: HV battery ECU internal fault or communication error
diagnostic_procedure: Check HV battery ECU power supply and communication.
Inspect HV battery wiring harness and orange HV connectors.
Replace HV battery ECU if internal fault confirmed.
Check HV battery charge and condition.
dtc_codes.pak_note: 12V battery (under bonnet, separate from HV battery) is a common cause. Replace 12V battery every 3 years.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Engine unable to crank or start in hybrid mode
diagnostic_procedure: Check 12V auxiliary battery first – most common cause.
Check engine start relay in hybrid system.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Dead 12V battery prevents Prius from starting – very common in Pakistan heat.

diagnostic_procedure: Note: On Prius Atkinson cycle engine, misfires affect HV charging.
Replace catalyst – common on older Pakistan Prius units.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common on 10+ year old Prius imports.

diagnostic_procedure: Re-register key transponder with Toyota Techstream.
For JDM imports: immobiliser re-registration mandatory after import.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Mandatory after JDM import to Pakistan.

dtc_codes.description: Low Power Supply Voltage to Skid Control ECU
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Supply voltage to ABS/skid control ECU low
diagnostic_procedure: Check ABS ECU power supply fuse.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Weak 12V battery causes this on Prius.

vehicles.pak_note: Very popular JDM kei car import. CVT fluid is ALMOST NEVER changed by Pakistani importers – this causes CVT damage. KF engine is reliable. Requires compatible OBD scanner (Daihatsu/Perodua compatible).

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: MAF signal implausible for engine conditions
dtc_codes.pak_note: Very common – dusty Pakistan roads.
Use OEM kei car spec spark plugs.

diagnostic_procedure: Check cyl 1 plug, coil, injector, compression.
Check cyl 2 plug, coil, injector, compression.
Check cyl 3 plug, coil, injector, compression.
Check CKP sensor wiring and gap.

dtc_codes.pak_note: Common on imported Miras.

diagnostic_procedure: Check fuel cap (kei car caps can be problematic).

dtc_codes.description: Torque Converter Clutch / CVT Clutch Slip
diagnostic_procedure: Change CVT fluid (Daihatsu CVT Fluid MULTI spec or equivalent).
Verify correct fluid type – wrong fluid destroys CVT.
Test CVT lockup clutch operation after fluid change.
If slip persists: CVT mechanical inspection required.
dtc_codes.pak_note: CRITICAL: Pakistani importers NEVER change CVT fluid on Mira. Old CVT fluid (dark brown) is the cause of almost all Mira CVT faults. Change CVT fluid immediately after purchase – it is a mandatory service item.
Old CVT fluid causes ratio faults.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: ISC valve circuit low voltage or no signal
diagnostic_procedure: Clean throttle body and ISC passage with throttle cleaner.
Perform throttle body adaptation after cleaning.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common after 40,000 km – carbon buildup in ISC passage.

diagnostic_procedure: Re-register key transponder with Daihatsu-compatible tool.
For JDM imports: immobiliser re-registration required after import.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Mandatory re-registration after JDM import to Pakistan. Many Miras arrive with this code active.

vehicles.pak_note: Very common JDM import in Pakistan. Typically 8–12 years old. CVT fluid neglect is #1 service issue. 1KR-FE is a very reliable small engine. Common issues are MAF sensor, O2 sensor, and CVT fluid maintenance.

dtc_codes.pak_note: Dusty conditions – clean MAF regularly.
Use NGK spec plugs only.
1.0L 3-cyl 1KR engine.

diagnostic_procedure: Check CKP sensor wiring and resistance.

dtc_codes.pak_note: Common on older Vitz.

diagnostic_procedure: Check CVT fluid (Toyota T-IV or CVT Fluid FE spec).
Drain and refill if fluid is dark.
dtc_codes.pak_note: CVT fluid almost never changed on Pakistan Vitz imports. Change immediately on purchase.

diagnostic_procedure: Change CVT fluid with Toyota genuine spec.
Test CVT pressure after fluid change.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Old CVT fluid is root cause.

diagnostic_procedure: Required for all JDM imports to Pakistan.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Mandatory re-registration for all imported Vitz.

vehicles.pak_note: High-prestige vehicle in Pakistan used by executives and government. Most are JDM or GCC imports. Diesel variant (1VD-FTV) is popular. Requires Toyota Techstream for full diagnostics. Common issues include VVT, EGR (diesel), injector balance, and ATF maintenance.

diagnostic_procedure: Perform OCV duty cycle test with Techstream.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Oil neglect causes VVT codes even on Land Cruiser.
V6/V8 Bank 2.
V6/V8 only.

diagnostic_procedure: Replace OEM iridium spark plugs (all 6 or 8 cylinders).
dtc_codes.pak_note: All 6 or 8 plugs should be replaced together.
V6/V8 only.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Igniter primary circuit fault – cylinder group 1
Igniter primary circuit fault – cylinder group 2

diagnostic_procedure: Check upstream and downstream O2 sensors.

dtc_codes.pak_note: V6/V8 only.

dtc_codes.description: Fuel Rail Pressure Too Low (1VD-FTV Diesel)
diagnostic_procedure: Replace fuel filter (diesel – change every 15,000 km in Pakistan).
dtc_codes.pak_note: Pakistan diesel quality is variable. Use clean diesel and replace fuel filter every 15,000 km.

dtc_codes.description: Turbocharger Boost Control Solenoid Circuit Range/Performance (1VD-FTV)
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Boost control solenoid signal out of range
diagnostic_procedure: Check boost solenoid wiring and resistance.
dtc_codes.pak_note: 1VD-FTV has twin turbo – check both boost solenoids.

dtc_codes.description: Turbocharger Boost Control Solenoid A Circuit Low
diagnostic_procedure: Check solenoid A circuit for short to ground.

dtc_codes.description: Turbocharger Boost Control Solenoid A Circuit High
diagnostic_procedure: Check solenoid A circuit for short to battery.
Inspect VNT turbocharger vane position actuator.
Clean EGR valve and cooler passages (very common on Land Cruiser diesel in Pakistan).
dtc_codes.pak_note: EGR carbon clogging is VERY COMMON on LC diesel with Pakistan diesel quality. Full EGR cleaning every 40,000 km.

diagnostic_procedure: Remove and thoroughly clean EGR valve.
Scan for individual cylinder injector codes.
Perform injector balance test with Techstream.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Dirty Pakistan diesel can contaminate injectors.
Use Toyota WS ATF. Change every 60,000 km.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: BARO sensor signal out of range
diagnostic_procedure: Measure sensor output vs. known atmospheric pressure.

vehicles.pak_note: Popular SUV in Pakistan upper-middle and affluent classes. 1GR-FE V6 petrol is the most common in Pakistan. 1KD-FTV diesel has EGR and injector issues. GCC/UAE imports are common.

diagnostic_procedure: Clean VVT OCV solenoid screens (Bank 1 and Bank 2).

dtc_codes.pak_note: V6 Bank 2.

diagnostic_procedure: Check air filter (large V6 requires clean filter for correct MAF).
Check Bank 2 vacuum hoses and injectors.

dtc_codes.pak_note: V6 only.
V6 only.
Pakistan diesel quality causes this – change fuel filter every 15,000 km.

diagnostic_procedure: Clean EGR valve – carbon clogging is severe on Prado diesel in Pakistan.
dtc_codes.pak_note: EGR cleaning is routine on Prado diesel in Pakistan. Clean every 40,000 km.

dtc_codes.description: Fuel Rail Pressure Sensor Circuit High (1KD-FTV)
diagnostic_procedure: Replace sensor if shorted to battery.

vehicles.pak_note: Luxury class vehicles in Pakistan – mostly used C-Class and E-Class imports. Mercedes requires XENTRY/DAS software for full diagnostics. Many codes are Mercedes-proprietary and not accessible with generic OBD2 scanners. Common issues include AIRMATIC suspension (where fitted), chain tensioner on M271/M274, and 7G-Tronic ATF.

dtc_codes.description: Intake Camshaft Position Timing Over-Advanced Bank 1
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Camshaft position timing deviation from target
diagnostic_procedure: Inspect timing chain tensioner – M271 is known for timing chain failure.
dtc_codes.pak_note: M271 1.8T has a known timing chain tensioner issue. If clicking at startup on M271, inspect chain immediately.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: CKP vs exhaust CMP correlation fault
dtc_codes.pak_note: Indicates possible chain stretch.

diagnostic_procedure: Check fuel pressure (check fuel pressure regulator on M271).
Check EVAP purge valve for sticking open.
Replace OEM Bosch or NGK spark plugs (all cylinders).
Check for chain stretch causing timing misfire.
Check boost control solenoid (N75 equivalent in Mercedes).
dtc_codes.pak_note: M271/M274 turbo only.
Intercooler hose cracks common.

diagnostic_procedure: Clean EGR valve and cooler (OM651/OM642 diesel).
dtc_codes.pak_note: EGR carbon clogging common on Mercedes diesel in Pakistan.

diagnostic_procedure: Check Mercedes MB236.14 or MB236.15 ATF level.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Mercedes 7G-Tronic requires MB236.14 specific ATF. NEVER use generic ATF. 7G-Tronic is sealed but valve body issues are common on high-mileage units.

diagnostic_procedure: Check turbine speed sensor wiring in transmission.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: CMP signal implausible or out of phase with CKP
diagnostic_procedure: Test CMP sensor signal with XENTRY oscilloscope function.
dtc_codes.pak_note: M271 timing chain wear is a known issue. If CMP codes appear, inspect chain immediately.

dtc_codes.description: Secondary Air Injection System Control Valve Circuit
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Secondary air injection valve circuit fault
diagnostic_procedure: Check secondary air injection control valve solenoid.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Common on older M271 C-Class.

dtc_codes.description: HRA (Headlamp Range Adjustment) – Supply Voltage Too Low
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: HRA module supply voltage below threshold
diagnostic_procedure: Inspect HRA module power supply fuse.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Requires XENTRY to read and clear.

diagnostic_procedure: Battery voltage check – Mercedes has many CAN-connected modules.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Mercedes has up to 47 ECUs – CAN faults can cascade. Battery replacement often required.

vehicles.pak_note: Prestigious brand in Pakistan. Mostly used imports – E90 3-Series and E60 5-Series are common. Requires BMW ISTA/ISTA+ or INPA software for full diagnostics. Generic OBD2 scanners miss most BMW-specific codes. VANOS faults, N20 timing chain, and N55 turbo are common.

dtc_codes.description: Intake (A) Camshaft Position Actuator Circuit Open Bank 1
Intake Camshaft Position Timing Over-Advanced Bank 1
diagnostic_procedure: Change engine oil (BMW LL-01 spec 5W-30 or 0W-30).
Note: N20 engine has a KNOWN timing chain tensioner issue – check immediately.
dtc_codes.pak_note: N20 engine timing chain is a significant known issue. Chain can jump on cold start causing expensive damage. Check at any sign of rattling.
N52/N55/N20 with dual VANOS.
N20 timing chain failure risk – inspect chain.

diagnostic_procedure: Note: BMW MAF sensors are sensitive to cleaning – use only electrical contact cleaner.
Check all rubber intake boots for cracks.
Inspect intake manifold for vacuum leaks.
Check PCV (crankcase ventilation) for blockage or failure.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Cracked intake boots are very common on older BMW in Pakistan due to heat.
N52/N55 V6 only.

diagnostic_procedure: Replace OEM NGK or Bosch spark plugs (all cylinders).
Check Bosch COP ignition coils – swap to identify.
Check for VANOS-related timing issues causing misfire.

dtc_codes.pak_note: N52/N55/B58 6-cyl only.
6-cyl only.
N20/N55/B47 turbo engines.

diagnostic_procedure: Check charge pipes for cracks – common on N55 in Pakistan heat.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Charge pipe cracks on N55 are common. Aftermarket pipes available but OEM replacement recommended.
B47 diesel EGR needs cleaning with Pakistan diesel quality.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Post-cat O2 activity high relative to pre-cat
diagnostic_procedure: Check upstream and downstream O2 sensors.

dtc_codes.pak_note: 6-cyl engines.

diagnostic_procedure: Scan ZF transmission TCM with ISTA.
Check ZF LifeGuard 6 ATF (ZF6HP) or LifeGuard 8 ATF (ZF8HP).
ZF8HP: Check mechatronic unit – common fault point.
dtc_codes.pak_note: BMW ZF8HP transmission is superb but requires ZF-spec ATF (LifeGuard 8). NEVER use generic ATF. Mechatronic unit failures are common on high-mileage E60/F10.

dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: Gear ratio fault in ZF transmission

diagnostic_procedure: Check turbine sensor in ZF transmission.
Inspect wiring harness inside transmission pan.

dtc_codes.description: Variable Valve Timing (VANOS) Control Circuit Bank 1 – Control Deviation
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: VANOS actuator not reaching commanded position
diagnostic_procedure: Check VANOS solenoid operation with ISTA.
Inspect VANOS actuator for wear or seizure.
Change engine oil – sludge from old oil prevents VANOS movement.
dtc_codes.pak_note: Clean VANOS system with engine oil flush if significant oil neglect has occurred.

dtc_codes.description: Variable Valve Timing (VANOS) Actuator Range/Performance Bank 1
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: VANOS actuator response out of spec
diagnostic_procedure: Inspect VANOS actuator vanes for wear.

dtc_codes.description: Variable Valve Timing (VVT) Control Module Bank 2 – RAM Error
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: DME RAM error related to VVT control
dtc_codes.pak_note: Requires BMW ISTA.

dtc_codes.description: Variable Valve Timing Bank 1 – Overload Protection/Overheat
Camshaft Position Sensor A Bank 1 – Incorrect Phase Setting
dtc_codes.malfunction_criteria: CMP sensor phase relationship out of specification
dtc_codes.pak_note: N20 timing chain concern.

dtc_codes.description: Phase Synchronisation System A Bank 1 – Solenoid Valve Position Implausible
diagnostic_procedure: Clean VANOS solenoid and filter screen.
Check CAN bus wiring (PT-CAN between DME, TCM, EGS).
dtc_codes.pak_note: BMW has multiple CAN buses – requires ISTA to identify which bus is affected.
